stories.headline: Ukraine war briefing: Zelenskyy reiterates truce offer ahead of Orthodox Easter
stories.summary: Ukrainian president says Russia unlikely to accept – ‘for them, nothing is sacred’; Australian police arrest army reservist for joining war. stories.summary:
Bloomberg Daybreak Europe is your essential morning viewing to stay ahead. Live from London, we set the agenda for your day, catching you up with overnight markets news from the US and Asia.

stories.headline: Iran war live: Trump warns of attacks as Hormuz deal deadline nears
stories.summary: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth says US forces are carrying out more strikes on Iran than any day since start of war.

stories.headline: How Pakistan’s solar boom is shielding it from worst of Iran war crisis
stories.summary: A quarter of Pakistani households are now using solar panels. This insulates millions of families from the energy crisis

stories.summary: Also in this newsletter: US President JD Vance travels to Hungary to support Viktor Orbán for the general elections. UN Goodwill Ambassador and actress Kristin Davis talks about the war in Sudan.

stories.summary: Ben Roberts-Smith, who denies all wrongdoing, previously lost a landmark defamation case over the alleged murders.

stories.headline: JD Vance due in Hungary to back Orban's re-election bid
stories.summary: The US vice-president's visit is the latest show of White House support for the Hungarian leader.

stories.headline: Quilliam: GCC Look for Supplementary Partners Post War
stories.summary: President Donald Trump ramped up his threats ahead of a deadline he’s imposed for Iran to re-open the Strait of Hormuz or face further attacks on civilian infrastructure. Meanwhile Tehran rejected a ceasefire proposal. Neil Quilliam, Associate Fellow Middle East & North Africa Programme at Chatham House spoke to Bloomberg's Horizons Middle East and Africa anchor Joumanna Bercetche on the Gulf nation's response to the war. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.summary: The US vice-president and Hungary’s prime minister will hold a joint press conference later todayMeanwhile, the opposition leader Péter Magyar has responded to JD Vance’s visit rejecting what he described as “interference in Hungarian elections.”He said:“No foreign country may interfere in Hungarian elections. This is our country. Hungarian history is not written in Washington, Moscow, or Brussels – it is written in Hungary’s streets and squares.

stories.headline: ‘Why would Iran agree to a ceasefire given US and Israeli track record?’
stories.summary: Political analyst Trita Parsi says no one should be surprised that Iran has rejected the idea of a US ceasefire deal.

stories.headline: ‘They want to create a rift’: Israeli attacks deepen Lebanon fissures
stories.summary: Lebanon grows increasingly divided as escalating Israeli bombardment and ground invasion worsen displacement crisis.

stories.headline: Europe Today: Vance rallies behind Orbán in Hungary election campaign’s final week
stories.summary: Tune in to Europe Today, Euronews' flagship morning programme at 8 am Brussels time. In just 20 minutes, we bring you up to speed on the biggest news of the day.

stories.headline: India's high-growth economy gets a Middle East oil shock
stories.summary: India's currency, stocks and growth projections take a beating as the country faces a triple energy shock due to Iran war.

stories.headline: Bill Ackman’s Pershing Square offers €55bn to buy Universal Music Group
stories.summary: Proposed deal would combine world’s largest music group with a blank-cheque company

stories.headline: Ackman’s Pershing Square Proposes Universal Music Merger
stories.summary: Pershing Square has proposed a combination with Universal Music that would move the listing into a US-based acquisition vehicle. Bill Ackman’s fund said the deal will value the world’s biggest music label at a 78% premium to its last closing price. Bloomberg's Neil Campling reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: ECB’s Elderson Presses Green-Transition Pitch After Energy Surge
stories.summary: Surging energy costs due to the war in the Middle East are the latest reminder for Europe to urgently reduce its reliance on fossil fuels, one of the European Central Bank’s top officials wrote in an op-ed published by the Financial Times.

stories.headline: India Bank Options Hedging Costs Climb on RBI, Geopolitical Risk
stories.summary: The cost of hedging against swings in a key Indian banking gauge relative to the broader market has surged, as traders brace for the central bank’s rate decision as well as a potential escalation in the Middle East war.

stories.headline: India Returns to Buying Venezuelan Crude to Ease Supply Crunch
stories.summary: India is set to import the most oil from Venezuela in almost six years, helping the world’s third-largest crude importer replace Middle East grades disrupted by the Iran war.

stories.headline: Air India CEO Campbell Wilson to Step Down Amid Mounting Losses
stories.summary: Air India Chief Executive Officer Campbell Wilson has resigned, people familiar with the matter said, as the Tata Group-owned carrier confronts record losses and operational setbacks.

stories.headline: Oil Rises as Trump Escalates Iran Threats
stories.summary: Oil rose a third day as US President Donald Trump escalated threats to obliterate key Iranian infrastructure if his terms aren’t met before a Tuesday deadline. Bloomberg's Anthony di Paola reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Australia charges ex-soldier Roberts-Smith with Afghan war crimes
stories.summary: Charges come after Roberts-Smith lost case against reporters who said he was involved in murders of unarmed Afghan men.

stories.headline: Trading Ban Upends Biggest Indian Bank’s $5 Billion Rupee Short
stories.summary: India’s biggest bank had about $5 billion of bets against the rupee that were impacted by the regulator’s crackdown on potential speculators, according to people with knowledge of the matter, roughly 20% of the total exposure.

stories.headline: LG Energy Posts Bigger-Than-Expected Loss as EV Aid Fades
stories.summary: LG Energy Solution Ltd. reported preliminary first-quarter earnings that missed analyst estimates, as waning electric-vehicle support in key markets like the US weighed on results, failing to offset strong demand for energy storage systems.

stories.headline: Indonesia Reforms Seen Averting MSCI Cut, Not Weighting Hit
stories.summary: Indonesian market reforms to meet MSCI Inc.’s demands may stave off a downgrade though they don’t go far enough to prevent a lower weighting in global indexes, some analysts said.

stories.headline: Trump Calls Artemis II Astronauts After Their Historic Journey Around the Moon
stories.summary: The conversation celebrated a small, but significant, step in an ambitious plan for missions to the moon and Mars that Mr. Trump had set early in his first term.

stories.headline: How Trump’s Endorsement in California Could Backfire Against Republicans
stories.summary: President Trump endorsed Steve Hilton, a Republican, in the governor’s race, which could help Democrats avoid being shut out of the general election.

GDP per capita in purchasing power: Which were Europe's richest and poorest countries in 2025?
stories.summary: Gross domestic product per capita in purchasing power standards varies significantly across Europe in 2025. One in three people in the EU lives in a country where GDP per capita in PPS is above the EU average.

stories.headline: Trump warns Iran it could be destroyed 'in one night' as deadline nears
stories.summary: Donald Trump has once again escalated threats against Iran, warning the country could be “taken out in one night” as the US deadline for Tehran approaches. Iran has rejected a proposed ceasefire, insisting on a permanent end to the war, while fighting continues to intensify across the region.

stories.headline: Gold Holds Steady as Traders Weigh Trump Deadline to Strike Iran
stories.summary: Gold held steady, following a two-day decline, as traders weighed US President Donald Trump’s latest threat to destroy Iranian infrastructure and the impact of a protracted war on economic growth.

stories.headline: Trump says US could charge for Strait of Hormuz passage amid Iran war
stories.summary: US president says Washington, as the 'winner' of the war, has a 'concept' for charging a toll in the strategic waterway.

stories.headline: Ten killed in Israeli strikes and clashes between Hamas and militia in Gaza, local sources say
stories.summary: The Israeli strikes reportedly targeted Hamas security personnel after they clashed with an Israeli-backed militia near Maghazi camp.

stories.headline: Trump threatens to take out Iran in 'one night' if no deal before deadline
stories.summary: The US president wants Tehran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global energy route, before his Tuesday deadline.

stories.headline: Trump issues expletive-laden threat to Iran over Hormuz Strait blockage
stories.summary: The US president says he will destroy Iranian power plants and bridges if the vital waterway is not reopened.

stories.headline: Trump declares victory after rescue but threats to US operation in Iran still loom
stories.summary: The rescue could impact how Trump views a ground operation to take Kharg Island or to seize enriched uranium sites.

stories.headline: Stocks Fall and Oil Climbs Before Iran Deadline: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: US stock futures fell and crude oil advanced ahead of President Donald Trump’s deadline for Iran to make a peace deal, with tentative ceasefire signals tempered by the risk of further escalation.

stories.summary: Stephen K. Bannon, a former close aide to President Trump, was convicted for failing to comply with a congressional subpoena related to the investigation into the Jan. 6 attack.

stories.headline: US Supreme Court clears path for Steve Bannon criminal case dismissal
stories.summary: Steve Bannon, an ally of US President Donald Trump, served a four-month prison sentence after his 2022 conviction.

stories.headline: Child rescued from rubble after Russia ramps up strikes on Ukraine

stories.summary: Oil rose a third day as President Donald Trump escalated threats to obliterate key Iranian infrastructure if his terms aren’t met before a Tuesday deadline.

stories.headline: When Suzuki met Suzuki: why a Tokyo dating agency is matching couples with the same name
stories.summary: Japan’s ban on married couples having different surnames has prompted an event to highlight people’s reluctance to change their nameAt the very least, the three men and three women calming their nerves on a Friday evening at a venue in Tokyo know they have one thing in common.Spaced out across booths, they will soon be placed in pairs and given 15 minutes to get to know one another. stories.summary: No one was injured, but the councilman, Ron Gibson, called it “deeply unsettling.”

stories.headline: Trump Administration Pulls Out of Civil Rights Settlements Backing Trans Students

stories.headline: The California Lake Billed as the ‘Saudi Arabia of Lithium’
stories.summary: Residents of Imperial County, Calif., are in dire need of an economic boost. Experts say the answer lies beneath the Salton Sea, where a lithium trove sits.

stories.headline: Hungary alleges plot to blow up gas pipeline ahead of election
stories.summary: It comes a week before votes are cast and follows warnings of so-called false flag operations blamed on Ukraine.

stories.headline: Spain's huge pork industry seeks salvation from swine fever threat

stories.headline: Russia chose 'Easter escalation' over ceasefire, says Zelensky
stories.summary: Six civilians were killed and 40 others injured as Russia launched hundreds of drones and missiles.

stories.headline: AI videos fuel rhetoric as Orbán bids for four more years in Hungary
stories.summary: Videos have targeted Viktor Orbán's election rival, who could unseat him after 16 years in office.

stories.headline: One killed and buildings damaged as magnitude 7.4 earthquake strikes Indonesia

Its epicentre was 127km (79 miles) west-north-west of Ternate, an

stories.headline: Magnitude 7.4 earthquake hits off Indonesia, killing one
stories.summary: The quake struck between the islands of Manado and Ternate. Tsunami warnings have now been lifted.

stories.headline: Fears cost of water and beer to soar as India's scorching summer hits
